label: Help Scout
description: Connect to Help Scout to read and manage conversations, mailboxes, customers, and tags from your shared inbox.

form.title: Connect Help Scout
form.subtitle: Paste your Help Scout App ID and App Secret to start pulling conversations and customer data.

methods.label: App ID + App Secret (OAuth 2.0 client credentials)
methods.description: Paste your Help Scout OAuth app credentials. Help Scout issues a bearer token automatically using the client_credentials grant — no browser pop-up required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via App ID and App Secret

Help Scout's API uses OAuth 2.0 with the `client_credentials` grant. You paste an App ID and App Secret; Anton exchanges them for a bearer token silently — no browser flow.

### Steps

1. Log in to Help Scout and open **Your Profile** (avatar, top-right).
2. Go to **My Apps → Create My App**.
3. Give the app a name like `Anton` and click **Create**.
4. Help Scout shows an **App ID** and **App Secret** on the app detail page.
5. Copy both values and paste them into the fields below.

### Notes

- The App Secret is shown immediately after creation. If you navigate away and lose it, click **Regenerate App Secret** on the same page — this invalidates the old one.
- Tokens issued via client_credentials are scoped to your Help Scout account. All mailboxes and conversations the account can access will be available to Anton.
- If your account belongs to multiple Help Scout companies, the token will reflect the company tied to the credentials you use.
- See [Help Scout API authentication docs](https://developer.helpscout.com/mailbox-api/overview/authentication/) for full details.

fields.name: app_id
fields.label: App ID
fields.description: Found on Your Profile → My Apps → (your app). Also called the OAuth client ID.

fields.name: app_secret
fields.label: App Secret
fields.description: Shown once after app creation. Regenerate it in Help Scout if you've lost it.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code — bring your own client)
methods.description: Opens a browser to let a Help Scout user grant Anton access interactively. Useful when you want per-user tokens rather than account-level credentials. Requires supplying your own OAuth app's client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow

This method opens Help Scout's consent screen in your browser. Use it when you want the token tied to a specific user's session rather than account-level credentials.

### Steps

1. Log in to Help Scout and go to **Your Profile → My Apps → Create My App**.
2. Set a **Redirect URL** — Anton will supply `http://127.0.0.1` (local loopback). Enter that or the URL Anton provides during setup.
3. After saving, copy the **App ID** (client ID) and **App Secret** (client secret) shown on the app page.
4. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.
5. A browser window opens to Help Scout's authorization screen. Log in and click **Authorize**.
6. Anton captures the authorization code and exchanges it for an access token.

### Notes

- Help Scout does not issue refresh tokens for the authorization code flow, so the session will expire after the access token lifetime. You may need to re-authorize periodically.
- Help Scout OAuth does not use explicit scopes in the authorization URL — access level is determined by the authenticated user's account permissions.
- The App Secret is shown once — save it before leaving the app creation page.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: App ID (Client ID)
fields.description: From Your Profile → My Apps → (your app). Used as the OAuth client_id.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: App Secret (Client Secret)
fields.description: Paired with the App ID. Shown once on app creation in Help Scout.
